title: I think you might be fooling yourself with AI
excerpt: If you are enthusiastic about AI (LLMs), you may be fooling yourself. Do not consider this an insult, but a kind-hearted warning.
content_html:
If you are enthusiastic about AI (LLMs), you may be fooling yourself. Do not consider this an insult, but a kind-hearted warning.

Remember what Richard Feynman said:

The first principle is that you must not fool yourself
and you are the easiest person to fool.

source (backup)

He made this statement as part of the Caltech's 1974 commencement address. In this speech he strongly advocated for proper scientific integrity.

I feel it can be difficult to maintain proper 'scientific integrity' on a personal level as AI is a fast-moving target. The tools feel unreasonably capable, using an LLM makes you feel more productive when writing code for instance.

Yet as of today, there is no independent scientific evidence1 (that I'm aware of) that people or organizations are indeed more productive2 when using AI.

Are you really more 'productive'?

Meanwhile, a study (late 2025) seems to report that although participating developers felt they completed tasks faster using AI, they where around 19% slower.

Using AI may feel faster and more productive, but you might be fooling yourself. You can never go back in time and measure yourself doing the task without using AI tools and compare the results.

There is a reason why (scientific) experiments are often blind or double-blind. We want prevent personal bias from influencing the outcomes skewing the results and making us believe things that aren't true. Not so easy to do on your own.

This is why I'm not really swayed by personal testimonies and anecdotes about how AI makes people more productive. How they vibe coded some app they would otherwise not have built3.

Do not be fooled by the mass layoffs 'due to AI' proving that AI is making people more productive. Sam Altman himself called many of these layoffs 'AI washing'. These are regular layoffs after a period of over-hiring and now the market corrects itself. Because aside from AI-related businesses, it seems that the economy isn't doing great.

Furthermore, some of these layoffs seem to be specifically to free up money to buy AI resources. Management by fear of being left behind. Firing people to get money to pay for unproven technology that may never be capable to replace the people being fired.

AI is unsustainable

Even if you still think AI is really making you more 'productive', enjoy it while it lasts.

According to this article ChatGPT starts losing money when you use your $200 subscription abouve 11%. If you'd use the full 100%, that same $200 subscription would allow you to use $14,000 worth in API pricing. That seems crazy to me.

Whatever you think your productivity might be due to AI, I don't think it's worth the 'real' cost once the vendors stop heavily subsidizing AI usage. I really wonder how much usage AI would see if it would be just priced at cost. Would you feel as productive if the price would not be $200 but $2000 a month4? A $200 subscription is not that big of a gamble, but any higher and it may feel less and less worth it.

Disclosure: my own bias against AI

Now it's time to be really open about my own bias. I'm an AI sceptic. I'm quite influenced by Ed Zitron's reporting. And I'm also standing by my own idea that AI will run out of money and just be turned off due to the huge operational cost.

It might feel unfathomable, unreasonable that this can happen. Maybe Google, AWS or Microsoft scoops up OpenAI or Antropic when they reach the end of the runway. But the economics won't change. Are they going to subsidize AI indefinitely?, to what end?

I'm open to suggestions how this is going to play out. Close to a trillion dollars invested in AI. Where will the money come from to recuperate all these investments? If we disregard the AI (adjacent) companies, the USA economy seems to look bleak (August 2025).

I myself can't just close my eyes for all the externalities of AI. The energy usage in the context of climate change. The data centers disrupting poor communities. The large-scale theft of intellectual or copyrighted property. The enshitification and the enslopification of the internet. The mass hoarding of memory causing inflation.

I'm not convinced the bad of AI is - or can be - compensated by the perceived good AI does. It's not possible to separate the tool itself from it's externalities. This is why I'm not using AI. I'm not using AI on moral, ethical grounds.

Hacker News discussion: here

Post Script

I found the comments on hacker news quite revealing. Most are very dismissive without engaging at all with the actual point of this article.

It is true that the cited Metr study was followed up with a more recent study from 2026 that showed more productivity when using AI. The authors are also pointing out they paid way less and they had a problem where developers didn't want to code without AI.

What was actually interesting about the initial study is the discrepancy between what developers thought and what they measured in terms of productivity. They were measurably fooling themselves.

Furthermore, it seems that people are quick to respond and not actually read and comprehend what I wrote. There are two key points I'm making:

  1. Your perception of productivity may be off / you may be fooling yourself
  2. In addition, I question and you may fool yourself thinking that the AI tool is worth the unsubsidized cost in relation to the perceived productivity gain.

At least I will try to be more explicit in my writing to get this point across.

I must say I'm amazed at how fast people dismiss this blog post and make claims about AI productivity, without anyone providing anything else but personal anekdotes.

  1. This may be due to my confirmation bias that I was unable to find any. ↩

  2. Whatever productive really means in this context. ↩

  3. how much value it delivers or how much revenue it creates is often not discussed. And don't fall for suvivorship bias. ↩

  4. Or would you get stressed out and feel pressure to use the AI more to justify the expense and burn out? ↩

blocks.html_en: If you are enthusiastic about AI (LLMs), you may be fooling yourself. Do not consider this an insult, but a kind-hearted warning.

sentences.en: If you are enthusiastic about AI (LLMs), you may be fooling yourself.
sentences.zh: 如果您对人工智能（法学硕士）充满热情，您可能是在欺骗自己。

sentences.en: Do not consider this an insult, but a kind-hearted warning.
sentences.zh: 不要认为这是一种侮辱，而是一种善意的警告。

blocks.html_en: Remember what Richard Feynman said:

sentences.en: Remember what Richard Feynman said:
sentences.zh: 记住理查德·费曼说过的话：

sentences.en: He made this statement as part of the Caltech's 1974 commencement address.
sentences.zh: 他在 1974 年加州理工学院毕业典礼演讲中发表了这一声明。

sentences.en: In this speech he strongly advocated for proper scientific integrity.
sentences.zh: 在这次演讲中，他强烈主张适当的科学诚信。

blocks.html_en: I feel it can be difficult to maintain proper 'scientific integrity' on a personal level as AI is a fast-moving target. The tools feel unreasonably capable, using an LLM makes you feel more productive when writing code for instance.

sentences.en: I feel it can be difficult to maintain proper 'scientific integrity' on a personal level as AI is a fast-moving target.
sentences.zh: 我觉得在个人层面上保持适当的“科学完整性”可能很困难，因为人工智能是一个快速变化的目标。

sentences.en: The tools feel unreasonably capable, using an LLM makes you feel more productive when writing code for instance.
sentences.zh: 这些工具给人的感觉非常强大，例如，使用法学硕士可以让你在编写代码时感觉更有效率。

blocks.html_en: Yet as of today, there is no independent scientific evidence1 (that I'm aware of) that people or organizations are indeed more productive2 when using AI.

sentences.en: Yet as of today, there is no independent scientific evidence1 (that I'm aware of) that people or organizations are indeed more productive2 when using AI.
sentences.zh: 然而，截至今天，还没有独立的科学证据1（据我所知）表明人们或组织在使用人工智能时确实提高了生产力2。

blocks.html_en: Are you really more 'productive'?

sentences.en: Are you really more 'productive'?
sentences.zh: 你真的更有“生产力”吗？

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, a study (late 2025) seems to report that although participating developers felt they completed tasks faster using AI, they where around 19% slower.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, a study (late 2025) seems to report that although participating developers felt they completed tasks faster using AI, they where around 19% slower.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，一项研究（2025 年末）似乎报告称，尽管参与的开发人员认为他们使用 AI 更快地完成了任务，但实际速度却慢了约 19%。

blocks.html_en: Using AI may feel faster and more productive, but you might be fooling yourself. You can never go back in time and measure yourself doing the task without using AI tools and compare the results.

sentences.en: Using AI may feel faster and more productive, but you might be fooling yourself.
sentences.zh: 使用人工智能可能会感觉更快、更有效率，但你可能在欺骗自己。

sentences.en: You can never go back in time and measure yourself doing the task without using AI tools and compare the results.
sentences.zh: 如果不使用人工智能工具并比较结果，你永远无法回到过去来衡量自己完成任务的情况。

blocks.html_en: There is a reason why (scientific) experiments are often blind or double-blind. We want prevent personal bias from influencing the outcomes skewing the results and making us believe things that aren't true. Not so easy to do on your own.

sentences.en: There is a reason why (scientific) experiments are often blind or double-blind.
sentences.zh: （科学）实验经常是盲法或双盲法是有原因的。

sentences.en: We want prevent personal bias from influencing the outcomes skewing the results and making us believe things that aren't true.
sentences.zh: 我们希望防止个人偏见影响结果、扭曲结果并使我们相信不真实的事情。

sentences.en: Not so easy to do on your own.
sentences.zh: 自己做起来可没那么容易。

blocks.html_en: This is why I'm not really swayed by personal testimonies and anecdotes about how AI makes people more productive. How they vibe coded some app they would otherwise not have built3.

sentences.en: This is why I'm not really swayed by personal testimonies and anecdotes about how AI makes people more productive.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我并没有真正被关于人工智能如何提高人们生产力的个人证词和轶事所左右。

sentences.en: How they vibe coded some app they would otherwise not have built3.
sentences.zh: 他们如何编码一些他们原本不会构建的应用程序3。

blocks.html_en: Do not be fooled by the mass layoffs 'due to AI' proving that AI is making people more productive. Sam Altman himself called many of these layoffs 'AI washing'. These are regular layoffs after a period of over-hiring and now the market corrects itself. Because aside from AI-related businesses, it seems that the economy isn't doing great.

sentences.en: Do not be fooled by the mass layoffs 'due to AI' proving that AI is making people more productive.
sentences.zh: 不要被“人工智能导致”的大规模裁员所愚弄，这证明人工智能正在提高人们的生产力。

sentences.en: Sam Altman himself called many of these layoffs 'AI washing'.
sentences.zh: 萨姆·奥尔特曼本人将其中许多裁员称为“人工智能清洗”。

sentences.en: These are regular layoffs after a period of over-hiring and now the market corrects itself.
sentences.zh: 这些都是在一段时间的过度招聘之后的定期裁员，现在市场正在自我纠正。

sentences.en: Because aside from AI-related businesses, it seems that the economy isn't doing great.
sentences.zh: 因为除了人工智能相关的业务之外，经济似乎表现不佳。

blocks.html_en: Furthermore, some of these layoffs seem to be specifically to free up money to buy AI resources. Management by fear of being left behind. Firing people to get money to pay for unproven technology that may never be capable to replace the people being fired.

sentences.en: Furthermore, some of these layoffs seem to be specifically to free up money to buy AI resources.
sentences.zh: 此外，其中一些裁员似乎是专门为了腾出资金来购买人工智能资源。

sentences.en: Management by fear of being left behind.
sentences.zh: 通过害怕被抛在后面进行管理。

sentences.en: Firing people to get money to pay for unproven technology that may never be capable to replace the people being fired.
sentences.zh: 解雇员工是为了获得资金来支付未经证实的技术，而这些技术可能永远无法取代被解雇的人。

blocks.html_en: AI is unsustainable

sentences.en: AI is unsustainable
sentences.zh: 人工智能是不可持续的

blocks.html_en: Even if you still think AI is really making you more 'productive', enjoy it while it lasts.

sentences.en: Even if you still think AI is really making you more 'productive', enjoy it while it lasts.
sentences.zh: 即使您仍然认为人工智能确实让您变得更加“高效”，也请趁它持续的时候享受它。

blocks.html_en: According to this article ChatGPT starts losing money when you use your $200 subscription abouve 11%. If you'd use the full 100%, that same $200 subscription would allow you to use $14,000 worth in API pricing. That seems crazy to me.

sentences.en: According to this article ChatGPT starts losing money when you use your $200 subscription abouve 11%.
sentences.zh: 根据这篇文章，当您使用 200 美元的订阅费用大约 11% 时，ChatGPT 就开始亏损。

sentences.en: If you'd use the full 100%, that same $200 subscription would allow you to use $14,000 worth in API pricing.
sentences.zh: 如果您要使用全部 100%，那么同样的 200 美元订阅将允许您使用价值 14,000 美元的 API 定价。

sentences.en: That seems crazy to me.
sentences.zh: 这对我来说似乎很疯狂。

blocks.html_en: Whatever you think your productivity might be due to AI, I don't think it's worth the 'real' cost once the vendors stop heavily subsidizing AI usage. I really wonder how much usage AI would see if it would be just priced at cost. Would you feel as productive if the price would not be $200 but $2000 a month4? A $200 subscription is not that big of a gamble, but any higher and it may feel less and less worth it.

sentences.en: Whatever you think your productivity might be due to AI, I don't think it's worth the 'real' cost once the vendors stop heavily subsidizing AI usage.
sentences.zh: 无论你认为你的生产力可能归功于人工智能，我认为一旦供应商停止大量补贴人工智能的使用，它就不值得付出“实际”成本。

sentences.en: I really wonder how much usage AI would see if it would be just priced at cost.
sentences.zh: 我真的很想知道如果人工智能只按成本定价的话，它的使用量会是多少。

sentences.en: Would you feel as productive if the price would not be $200 but $2000 a month4?
sentences.zh: 如果价格不是每月 200 美元而是 2000 美元4，您会感觉工作效率高吗？

sentences.en: A $200 subscription is not that big of a gamble, but any higher and it may feel less and less worth it.
sentences.zh: 200 美元的订阅并不是一场多大的赌博，但如果更高，你可能会觉得越来越不值得。

sentences.zh: 披露：我自己对人工智能的偏见

sentences.en: The data centers disrupting poor communities.
sentences.zh: 数据中心扰乱了贫困社区。

sentences.en: The large-scale theft of intellectual or copyrighted property.
sentences.zh: 大规模盗窃知识产权或版权财产。

sentences.zh: 互联网的enshitification和enslopification。

sentences.en: The mass hoarding of memory causing inflation.
sentences.zh: 大量囤积内存导致通货膨胀。

sentences.zh: 我不相信人工智能的坏处可以——或者可以——被人们认为的人工智能的好处所弥补。

sentences.en: It's not possible to separate the tool itself from it's externalities.
sentences.zh: 不可能将工具本身与其外部性分开。

sentences.en: This is why I'm not using AI.
sentences.zh: 这就是我不使用人工智能的原因。

sentences.en: I'm not using AI on moral, ethical grounds.
sentences.zh: 我使用人工智能并不是出于道德、伦理的考虑。

sentences.en: It is true that the cited Metr study was followed up with a more recent study from 2026 that showed more productivity when using AI.

sentences.en: They were measurably fooling themselves.
sentences.zh: 他们在很大程度上是在自欺欺人。

blocks.html_en: Furthermore, it seems that people are quick to respond and not actually read and comprehend what I wrote. There are two key points I'm making:

sentences.en: Furthermore, it seems that people are quick to respond and not actually read and comprehend what I wrote.
sentences.zh: 此外，人们似乎很快就做出了回应，但并没有真正阅读和理解我所写的内容。

sentences.en: There are two key points I'm making:
sentences.zh: 我要强调的有两个要点：

blocks.html_en: • Your perception of productivity may be off / you may be fooling yourself

sentences.en: Your perception of productivity may be off / you may be fooling yourself
sentences.zh: 你对生产力的看法可能有问题/你可能在欺骗自己

blocks.html_en: • In addition, I question and you may fool yourself thinking that the AI tool is worth the unsubsidized cost in relation to the perceived productivity gain.

sentences.en: In addition, I question and you may fool yourself thinking that the AI tool is worth the unsubsidized cost in relation to the perceived productivity gain.
sentences.zh: 此外，我提出疑问，你可能会欺骗自己，认为与感知到的生产力提升相比，人工智能工具值得付出无补贴的成本。

blocks.html_en: At least I will try to be more explicit in my writing to get this point across.

sentences.en: At least I will try to be more explicit in my writing to get this point across.
sentences.zh: 至少我会尝试在写作中更加明确地表达这一点。

blocks.html_en: I must say I'm amazed at how fast people dismiss this blog post and make claims about AI productivity, without anyone providing anything else but personal anekdotes.

sentences.en: I must say I'm amazed at how fast people dismiss this blog post and make claims about AI productivity, without anyone providing anything else but personal anekdotes.
sentences.zh: 我必须说，我很惊讶人们如此迅速地驳回这篇博文并声称人工智能生产力，除了个人轶事之外没有任何人提供任何其他内容。

blocks.html_en: • This may be due to my confirmation bias that I was unable to find any. ↩

sentences.en: This may be due to my confirmation bias that I was unable to find any. ↩
sentences.zh: 这可能是由于我无法找到任何确认偏差。 ↩

blocks.html_en: This may be due to my confirmation bias that I was unable to find any. ↩

sentences.en: This may be due to my confirmation bias that I was unable to find any. ↩
sentences.zh: 这可能是由于我无法找到任何确认偏差。 ↩

blocks.html_en: • Whatever productive really means in this context. ↩

sentences.en: Whatever productive really means in this context. ↩
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，生产力的真正含义是什么。 ↩

blocks.html_en: Whatever productive really means in this context. ↩

sentences.en: Whatever productive really means in this context. ↩
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，生产力的真正含义是什么。 ↩

blocks.html_en: • how much value it delivers or how much revenue it creates is often not discussed. And don't fall for suvivorship bias. ↩

sentences.en: how much value it delivers or how much revenue it creates is often not discussed.
sentences.zh: 它提供了多少价值或创造了多少收入通常没有被讨论。

sentences.en: And don't fall for suvivorship bias. ↩
sentences.zh: 并且不要陷入幸存者偏见。 ↩

blocks.html_en: how much value it delivers or how much revenue it creates is often not discussed. And don't fall for suvivorship bias. ↩

sentences.en: how much value it delivers or how much revenue it creates is often not discussed.
sentences.zh: 它提供了多少价值或创造了多少收入通常没有被讨论。

sentences.en: And don't fall for suvivorship bias. ↩
sentences.zh: 并且不要陷入幸存者偏见。 ↩

blocks.html_en: • Or would you get stressed out and feel pressure to use the AI more to justify the expense and burn out? ↩

sentences.en: Or would you get stressed out and feel pressure to use the AI more to justify the expense and burn out? ↩
sentences.zh: 或者你会感到压力很大，并感到有压力需要更多地使用人工智能来证明费用合理并精疲力尽吗？ ↩

blocks.html_en: Or would you get stressed out and feel pressure to use the AI more to justify the expense and burn out? ↩

sentences.en: Or would you get stressed out and feel pressure to use the AI more to justify the expense and burn out? ↩
sentences.zh: 或者你会感到压力很大，并感到有压力需要更多地使用人工智能来证明费用合理并精疲力尽吗？ ↩

content_html_zh:
如果您对人工智能（法学硕士）充满热情，您可能是在欺骗自己。 不要认为这是一种侮辱，而是一种善意的警告。

记住理查德·费曼说过的话：

The first principle is that you must not fool yourself
and you are the easiest person to fool.

来源（备份）

他在 1974 年加州理工学院毕业典礼演讲中发表了这一声明。 在这次演讲中，他强烈主张适当的科学诚信。

我觉得在个人层面上保持适当的“科学完整性”可能很困难，因为人工智能是一个快速变化的目标。 这些工具给人的感觉非常强大，例如，使用法学硕士可以让你在编写代码时感觉更有效率。

然而，截至今天，还没有独立的科学证据1（据我所知）表明人们或组织在使用人工智能时确实提高了生产力2。

你真的更有“生产力”吗？

与此同时，一项研究（2025 年末）似乎报告称，尽管参与的开发人员认为他们使用 AI 更快地完成了任务，但实际速度却慢了约 19%。

使用人工智能可能会感觉更快、更有效率，但你可能在欺骗自己。 如果不使用人工智能工具并比较结果，你永远无法回到过去来衡量自己完成任务的情况。

（科学）实验经常是盲法或双盲法是有原因的。 我们希望防止个人偏见影响结果、扭曲结果并使我们相信不真实的事情。 自己做起来可没那么容易。

这就是为什么我并没有真正被关于人工智能如何提高人们生产力的个人证词和轶事所左右。 他们如何编码一些他们原本不会构建的应用程序3。

不要被“人工智能导致”的大规模裁员所愚弄，这证明人工智能正在提高人们的生产力。 萨姆·奥尔特曼本人将其中许多裁员称为“人工智能清洗”。 这些都是在一段时间的过度招聘之后的定期裁员，现在市场正在自我纠正。 因为除了人工智能相关的业务之外，经济似乎表现不佳。

此外，其中一些裁员似乎是专门为了腾出资金来购买人工智能资源。 通过害怕被抛在后面进行管理。 解雇员工是为了获得资金来支付未经证实的技术，而这些技术可能永远无法取代被解雇的人。

人工智能是不可持续的

即使您仍然认为人工智能确实让您变得更加“高效”，也请趁它持续的时候享受它。

根据这篇文章，当您使用 200 美元的订阅费用大约 11% 时，ChatGPT 就开始亏损。 如果您要使用全部 100%，那么同样的 200 美元订阅将允许您使用价值 14,000 美元的 API 定价。 这对我来说似乎很疯狂。

无论你认为你的生产力可能归功于人工智能，我认为一旦供应商停止大量补贴人工智能的使用，它就不值得付出“实际”成本。 我真的很想知道如果人工智能只按成本定价的话，它的使用量会是多少。 如果价格不是每月 200 美元而是 2000 美元4，您会感觉工作效率高吗？ 200 美元的订阅并不是一场多大的赌博，但如果更高，你可能会觉得越来越不值得。

披露：我自己对人工智能的偏见

现在是时候真正公开我自己的偏见了。 我是人工智能怀疑论者。 Ed Zitron 的报道对我影响很大。 我也坚持自己的观点，即人工智能将耗尽资金，并因巨大的运营成本而被关闭。

发生这种事可能会让人觉得不可思议、不合理。 当 OpenAI 或 Antropic 到达跑道尽头时，也许 Google、AWS 或 Microsoft 会抢占先机。 但经济不会改变。 他们会无限期地补贴人工智能吗？目的是什么？

我愿意接受关于这件事将如何进行的建议。 近万亿美元投资于人工智能。 资金从哪里来恢复所有这些投资？ 如果我们忽略人工智能（邻近）公司，美国经济似乎显得黯淡（2025 年 8 月）。

我自己不能对人工智能的所有外部性视而不见。 气候变化背景下的能源使用。 数据中心扰乱了贫困社区。 大规模盗窃知识产权或版权财产。 互联网的enshitification和enslopification。 大量囤积内存导致通货膨胀。

我不相信人工智能的坏处可以——或者可以——被人们认为的人工智能的好处所弥补。 不可能将工具本身与其外部性分开。 这就是我不使用人工智能的原因。 我使用人工智能并不是出于道德、伦理的考虑。

黑客新闻讨论：这里

后脚本

我发现有关黑客新闻的评论很有启发性。 大多数人都非常不屑一顾，根本没有关注本文的实际观点。

确实，所引用的 Metr 研究是在 2026 年进行的一项更新研究的后续研究，该研究表明使用人工智能时生产力更高。 作者还指出，他们支付的费用要少得多，而且他们遇到的问题是开发人员不想在没有人工智能的情况下进行编码。

最初的研究真正有趣的是开发人员的想法和他们在生产力方面测量的结果之间的差异。 他们在很大程度上是在自欺欺人。

此外，人们似乎很快就做出了回应，但并没有真正阅读和理解我所写的内容。 我要强调的有两个要点：

  1. 你对生产力的看法可能有问题/你可能在欺骗自己
  2. 此外，我提出疑问，你可能会欺骗自己，认为与感知到的生产力提升相比，人工智能工具值得付出无补贴的成本。

至少我会尝试在写作中更加明确地表达这一点。

我必须说，我很惊讶人们如此迅速地驳回这篇博文并声称人工智能生产力，除了个人轶事之外没有任何人提供任何其他内容。

  1. 这可能是由于我无法找到任何确认偏差。 ↩
  2. 在这种情况下，生产力的真正含义是什么。 ↩
  3. 它提供了多少价值或创造了多少收入通常没有被讨论。 并且不要陷入幸存者偏见。 ↩
  4. 或者你会感到压力很大，并感到有压力需要更多地使用人工智能来证明费用合理并精疲力尽吗？ ↩
